title: helianthemum cinereum
intro: Helianthemum cinereum, also known as rockrose, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and silvery-green foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Helianthemum cinereum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Helianthemum cinereum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used as a component in skin creams and salves to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
